content.full_text:
Law Offices
Michael Schaefer
Admitted to all courts
of California & Nevada, the
United States Supreme Court
and United States Tax Court

San Diego Office:
LA JOLLA CORPORATE CENTER
3252 Holiday Court, Suite 103
La Jolla, California 92037

Telephone(619) 558-4750                    Fax (619) 450-6786


April 17, 1992


Mr. Wayne Orthos (just guessing at your last name)
MFPC
    Fax 916 327-2026

    I apologize for any inconvenience or offense to your staff
when I called. I naively expect a public office to give some indication
that they received an inquiry and something is being done about it,
and until April 15, I did not know if you received my April 8th
inquiry.

    I wanted to simply call up and ask:

    1. Did our political funding iniative close the loophole of
candidates being able to keep a big warchest for the next election?

    2. Are there any other abuses in the way things are done that
personally, off-the-cuff, confidentially, appear to be ripe for
legislative consideration, that you could share with me——and I would
talk about them as personal potential things to do if elected.

    I expect you to simply say: Yes or No, to the query, and
"we cannot get into what the laws should be or what we'd like; we
are mandated only to enforce existing statutes".

    I did not get that in a phone call April 8. I'd hoped to
get it in a prompt return call or fax, and all of this week has
gone by.     Nothing in today's mail.

    If at some future time you have an answer for me, that would
be nice. Am not counting on it and will be speaking on other matters,
such as welfare fraud, and not get to political reform until I have
a couple of ideas to toss out.    I got some dandy ideas on welfare
reform after visiting with the District Attorney's welfare prosecutor.

Confidially, [illegible]

MICHAEL SCHAEFER
75th AD, Republican
Candidate


APR 17 '92 13:05                                           PAGE.001

Law Offices
Michael Schaefer
Admitted to all courts
of California & Nevada, the
United States Supreme Court
and United States Tax Court

San Diego Office:
LA JOLLA CORPORATE CENTER
3252 Holiday Court, Suite 103
La Jolla, California 92037

Telephone(619) 558-4750

Fax (619) 450-6786

Re-sent 4/10
no answer yet
please call w fax.

April 8, 1992

Mr. Wayne Orbos
FPPC
Fax 916  327-2026

Dear Sir:

I am interested in proposing closing of some loopholes in
our election laws, and I don't know if the following abuses have been
cured, or not; I am not asking you to agree that they are abuses or not,
but whether the conduct is still legal, and any other things that
the legislature might consider.  Campaign reform is only one of several
issues I discuss in my campaign, and I am sorry I have not had time to
sit down and talk these over at length:

1. End the Political Warchest.  Presently(?) incumbents can
amass unlimited funds in their warchest for future campaigns.  This
discourages any competition.  We should limit a campaign fund to
be turned over the the State Treasurer at end of an election year, or
when all bills are paid, whichever occurs first.  (I think a Proposition
took care of this?)

2. End Newsletter abuse.  Incumbents sometimes send 'newsletters'
weeks or months before the election, at public expense; we should ban any
bulk mailing from a legislature to his/her district within 6 months of
an election involving that lawmaker.

3. End Big-buck Campaigns.  Candidates have spent over $500,000
to win an Assembly seat.  (Millions???).  Let's limit costs, regardless of
source, to 50¢ per voter in the primary, and again in the general election,
and reduce the $1,000 current limit per-donor to $500;  Thank you.

Michael Schaefer, GOP candidate Assembly Dist.75

[signature]
